name: fd-experiment-feasibility
focus: Implementation feasibility of each experiment's technical spec within standalone HTML/JS constraint
persona: A frontend engineer evaluating whether each spec is achievable without a build system, bundler, or framework — just index.html and CDN imports.
decision_lens: Prioritizes findings where the spec makes implicit assumptions that break in file:// or CDN context (module resolution, CORS, SharedArrayBuffer).
review_areas: F3 Hydra: hydra-synth CDN availability, ES module import without bundler, full-screen WebGL standalone
F4 Cytoscape fCoSE: CDN path for extension, constraint API for outer-ring layout, registration via cytoscape.use()
F7 PM4JS: browser-compatible ES module build existence — if absent, D3 fallback realism
F6 Loopy: SVG particle animation with D3 at throughput-proportional rates, frame budget at 20+ particles
F5 Raster: Canvas with hover tooltip pixel-to-cell mapping, ring buffer accumulation matching 24-column grid
F2 Split-Flap: CSS-only animation with ::before/::after per-character, 20 agents x 60 chars concurrent animation
success_hints: Each experiment opens from file:// without local server (except F9)
CDN URLs for every library are pinned and discoverable
No experiment requires a build step
task_context: 10 standalone experiments for Cybersyn-inspired AI factory dashboard, each independently runnable index.html with no build toolchain.

name: fd-dependency-ordering
focus: Correctness of dependency graph, batch sequencing, and parallelization claims
persona: A technical program manager who maps stated dependencies to actual runtime coupling and identifies hidden sequencing constraints.
decision_lens: Prioritizes findings where a stated parallel experiment has a hidden dependency that would block, or critical path is misstated.
review_areas: F1 critical path: do F2-F7 truly depend on F1 being complete, or could they use hardcoded fixtures
F8 vs F1 coupling: runtime import vs compatible interface — could F8 develop against a stub
F9 extends F8: does F9 SSE diff logic depend on F8 client ring buffer implementation
Batch 2 parallelization: any shared utility code creating implicit merge conflict risk
F10 prerequisites: plugin interface (mount/unmount) must be agreed before visuals start — hidden ordering constraint
Commit strategy: batch boundaries vs dependency graph alignment
success_hints: Every parallel experiment can genuinely start immediately after stated prerequisite
Plugin interface contract defined before visual experiments begin
Dependency graph matches actual import relationships
task_context: 10 experiments in 4 batches with stated dependency graph. F1 is critical path. Six visuals claimed fully parallelizable.

name: fd-tech-stack-viability
focus: Practical viability of specific library choices for standalone browser prototypes
persona: A browser technology specialist evaluating CDN availability, browser compatibility, bundle size, and API fit for each library.
decision_lens: Prioritizes findings where a library's design assumptions conflict with experiment constraints — Node.js-first in browser, bundler required, or API mismatch.
review_areas: hydra-synth: CDN availability, AudioContext gesture unlock, programmatic API vs REPL-first design
cytoscape-fcose: CDN path, constraint API for positioned layout, license compatibility
PM4JS: browser-compatible build existence — if absent, D3 swim-lane token animation fallback
D3 version: v6 vs v7 event handling API differences affecting SVG particle animation
file:// CORS for ES module imports across sibling directories
TradingView Lightweight Charts: CDN availability and standalone embedding without React wrapper
success_hints: Every library has verified CDN URL serving ES module or UMD build
No library requires user gesture, special HTTP header, or server feature to initialize
PM4JS has confirmed browser build or explicit D3 fallback path
task_context: Experiment suite relies on specific third-party libraries from research. Each must work CDN-only, no-bundler, standalone HTML.

name: fd-scope-containment
focus: Risk of scope creep and whether experiment boundaries are sharp enough to stay standalone
persona: A senior engineer who identifies where prototype specs contain implicit features pulling in extra complexity, quietly violating the standalone constraint.
decision_lens: Prioritizes where a single experiment attempts multiple problems, criteria require infrastructure from another experiment, or spec implies UX polish beyond prototype.
review_areas: F7 scope: event log accumulation + state transition detection + scrub/play/pause + SVG/Canvas hybrid — one experiment or three?
F6 perturbation mode: second interaction mode on top of ambient viz — doubles implementation scope?
F4 interaction depth: click-highlight, dep-path, double-click-reset, live diff, animated transitions — prototype or feature?
F10 URL state: query params, history API, state serialization — composition experiment or production infra?
F2 sort+header: auto-sort by status plus header with live metrics — belongs in visual prototype or F10 shell?
F9 static file serving from ../ — couples to all experiment directory structure?
success_hints: Each experiment describable in one sentence without conjunctions
Criteria satisfiable without implementing features from another experiment
Most complex experiment has defined minimum viable subset
task_context: Plan keeps experiments standalone and parallelizable. Scope creep risks delaying batches, creating unplanned dependencies, or making composition harder.

name: fd-acceptance-criteria-quality
focus: Whether acceptance criteria are objectively testable, complete, and consistent with implementation specs
persona: A QA engineer evaluating whether each criterion can be verified by a third party without subjective judgment, and whether critical behaviors are missing.
decision_lens: Prioritizes subjective language in criteria, key behaviors with no corresponding criterion, and criteria impossible to verify without full system.
review_areas: F2 subjective: 'legible at 3m' — objectively verifiable? Split-flap animation correctness criterion missing?
F3 performance: '60fps on 2020-era laptop' — measurement method? Achievable on integrated graphics?
F4 missing criteria: hover labels and zoom/pan in spec but not in PRD acceptance criteria
F1 schema validity: 'matching factory-status schema' — schema defined or linked anywhere?
F9 diff correctness: testable definition of diff? When full vs partial?
F10 contradiction: PRD lists websocket in data toggle but websocket is a non-goal
success_hints: Every criterion verifiable by script or devtools — no subjective judgment
Every spec behavior has at least one acceptance criterion
No criterion references a non-goal feature
task_context: Plan separates specs (plan.md) from criteria (prd.md). Criteria used to close beads. Ambiguous or mismatched criteria block closure or allow incomplete implementations.

name: fd-composition-architecture
focus: Architectural risks in F10 composition shell — plugin interface, routing, lazy loading, data source switching
persona: A frontend architect who has built plugin-based shell applications. Evaluates whether the plugin contract, hash router, and lazy loading compose experiments built independently.
decision_lens: Prioritizes where plugin contract assumptions break across six independently developed experiments, and where data source switching creates lifecycle problems.
review_areas: Plugin interface: mount(container, dataPipe) sufficient for experiments with different init requirements (Hydra canvas, Cytoscape div dimensions)?
Lazy loading: dynamic import() for self-contained HTML files — need refactoring from inline init to mount() call?
Data pipe switching: unmount+remount with new DataPipe — experiments accumulating history (F5, F7) lose state?
Shared header collision: F2 has own header, shell has header — visual collision when F2 mounted in shell?
URL param namespace: flat query string mixes shell params and experiment params — collision risk?
Thumbnail strip: static images for generative/animated content — realistic?
success_hints: mount(container, dataPipe) implementable by all six visuals without modifying core rendering
Data source switch doesn't require full page reload or lose more than one update of history
Shell testable against two stubs before any real visual is complete
task_context: F10 is the integration point. Architectural flaws in plugin contract or lifecycle surface only when independently-developed experiments are first combined — at sprint end when least time to fix.
